title: All's well that ends well
content:
I liked using the Scottish Power website. Process of renewing for a further year was okay to follow and I read through the various options
However, just as I reached the stage of concluding the tariff for me next year, I had a message to say I could not continue and I should call the 0800 number, provided.
I worried about what had gone wrong but didn't call number till two days later as it was Easter holiday weekend, only to be told that my choice had gone through and all was well. I had no online evidence/confirmation of this.
Fortunately the agent I spoke with was very reassuring that all was well and he has arranged for me to receive  confirmation details which I look forward to receiving,

title: Brilliant Company
content:
I have been with Scottish Power for over a decade and I can't fault them. Their staff in their call centres are very friendly and very knowledgeable. They don't push products onto you - if you say no they accept it. 
But usually I can do everything I need online. Their app is great and allows you to manage your account, sign up for new things like the power saver and rewards and control your direct debit. It shows you your usage and I love that you can compare it to previous years. If you do need to speak to someone there is an online chat feature or the phone number. 
I love the power saver initiative where I can select 8 hours a week when I receive half price electricity.

title: Outrageous final bill for gas after exiting the service
content:
I switched from Scottish Power to another supplier because of back and forth on my gas account, on termination of service I was sent a final gas bill of £193 plus which has now increased to £208.44 from December 25th to January 26th. 
I called to let them know the bill is outrageous, they requested for a screenshot of the gas reading from the meter for 21st of January 2026 which I didn't take because they sent a mail on termination of the service that they would get the reading automatically. 
Now they insist they can't do anything to reduce the bill because I don't have a proof of the meter reading they requested for.

I called several times, none of the customer service rep who picked my call logged the complaint and create a ticket until the one I spoke to today.

It is really disheartening.

title: The worst company that exists
content: The worst company that exists. They charged me for 4 years of gas and electricity bills for flats that were not mine. Now I’ve discovered everything and I’m taking legal action against them. They put me under a lot of psychological pressure, with calls, letters, and emails demanding payment of bills in the name of “occupier.” They forced me to pay £8,000. title: On the whole they good
content: On the whole they provide a good service and prices are usually competitive. Via the website or app, customers have control over their direct debit amount and there are useful tools to keep track of energy usage and compare to previous years. I had some issues last year , but they were sorted out eventually to my satisfaction. Recently they changed the half price electricity weekend deal to give customers more choice of time slots to save. Sounded good on the face of it but I think Scottish Power think customers won’t notice that they’ve reduced it from 10 hours at the weekend to 8 hours . Well I have noticed ! That said it was useful to be able to choose a better time to save. I hope they continue to give us the best prices when energy costs rise in July.

Subsequently my bills look incorrect with the proportion of off peak and peak consumption not agreeing to my meter readings in the house. My ScottishPower App is showing consumption in the wrong half hour slots. We are now a year or so on and I just get passed from pillar to post. The information we need to unravel this I am told as it will “overload the system “.I have spent days of my time on this and have no confidence that it will get sorted. I believe I have to reach deadlock before I take the matter to the Small Claims Court and Ofgen.

title: Speechless
content:
Speechless. After more than a year of disputes, I’ve ended up with a credit mark and a debt collector involved—yet still no clear explanation, no proper resolution, and no accountability.

My winter reduction payment was received, but it has never appeared on my account. Despite raising this multiple times, nothing has been corrected.

To make matters worse, when I tried to pay the debt collector, the payment failed twice due to “technical issues.” On top of that, the amount requested by Richburn differs from what was shown on my original SP account—lower, but unexplained. This inconsistency raises serious concerns.

I have kept all evidence throughout this process. From my experience, this does not feel like simple error or mismanagement—it feels like a system that allows people to be misled and financially affected without proper oversight.

This situation has caused unnecessary stress and damage to my credit record. I expected professionalism, transparency, and basic accountability—none of which have been delivered.

I strongly urge this to be investigated and taken seriously so others do not go through the same experience.

title: Won't be referring anyone towards Scottish power and their shoddy customer services
content:
Ive had The same issue since December its now 30th March, all I needed to do was change direct debit dates, this would normally be straightforward of a job for any company except Scottish power.
Ive been on the phone a total of 8 different times and each phone call lasting over an hour to do this. I wanted to change from the 6th of each month to the 21st of each month due to changing jobs and the pay dates were different.
I thought this was sorted after the first 2 conversations but they went on to take payments on the 14th and then the 16th then after more conversations and more people telling me they have 100% sorted it they just didnt take payment at all. The amount of stress this has caused me has 100% made me realise I will never refer anyone to Scottish power, you could pardon a mistake once of twice due to the language barrier that was in hand but this much hassle for a simple objective is bone idle and lack of care from customer services. 
Then the last lady I spoke to she was worth her weight in gold, finally someone at Scottish power who cared, she didn't only manage to do the "impossible" of changing a direct debit date but she also managed to find a fault with my gas meter she jumped through all the hoops and processes to sort it out for me and got it sorted and scheduled a follow-up call to see if 1, the payment come out on the chosen date and 2, if the gas meter is up and running still. If Scottish power had more of that lady working with them they would have a long list of happy customers and achieve great things, instead ive had to go through multiple people who didn't care about me as a customer and had absolutely no enthusiasm towards what job they were clocked in to do and get paid for.

title: If I could give less than 1 star then I…
content:
If I could give less than 1 star then I would.

They are breathtakingly incompetent.

In the past 3 years I have spent a total of more than 6 hours on the phone trying to resolve an issue which is entirely caused by Scottish Power. The call centre staff are generally very pleasant and usually helpful. I get assured repeatedly that they understand the issue and they say that it is all written in the file for the next person to see but mostly that does not happen. I get further calls but the caller cannot see that I have been told the issue is resolved (It has not been). The cycle of confusion continues....just rinse and repeat, as it were. The final straw came today when I was contacted by solicitors chasing a debt but they had been instructed the same day that Scottish Power last assured me that there was no problem.

The Scottish Power account is in the name of my elderly sister who has dementia and Alzheimers but lives alone. She is on the S.P. VUlnerable Person list (but keeps getting removed from that). I have authorisation to deal with S.P. and hold a power of attorney. The customer has a direct debit arrangement in place and I have been repeatedly told by S.P. on the phone that they are happy and the account details are correct BUT when they issue bills, the bills say on them that the agreed amount will be taken on a particular date. The invoices say clearly "you do not need to do anything further". S.P. then fails to take the direct debit (despite assurances that they will) and so the debt builds up. I have repeatedly been told that the problem is solved. On 23rd March £588 was paid on a debit card at the request of S.P. who also agreed a new monthly direct debit amount to clear the outstanding debt....at an amount higher than what they were suggesting. The customer does not dispute the bills and is quite willing to pay by the direct debit that S.P. holds.....however, S.P. just fails to take the money owing and it is not the fault of the customer or her bank. So I believed that the matter was resolved by S.P. on 23rd of March when I spoke several times to a woman called Ndombe at their call centre in South Africa who totally assured me thaT Scottish Power was now happy and it had been all their fault. Later that  same day S.P. instructed Flint Bishop Solicitors to persue the customer despite telling me that the issue was resolved and had been the fault of Scottish Power. If this matter continues, I will not rest until it has been aired in the press and on TV consumer shows.

title: Be very wary about dealing with Scottish Power
content:
I have had the displeasure of being a customer of Scottish Power since 14 January, and can honestly say I have seen some of the worst administrational processes, harassment, and mis-management I have experienced as a customer. 
As the face of Scottish Power, the call centre really do not represent this company at all well (but read on). I have had 6 calls with the customer service advisors since 27 February to dispute an 'estimated' bill (for an unoccupied property. The bill is 2-times greater than my own home bill with a family of 4).  
I have been effectively misinformed twice about how the bill has been derived, and that my request to be escalated to the complaints department (on 6 March) has been actioned.
During my lengthy call on 6 March with the billing department, the operator was unable to explain how the bill had been calculated.  Additionally, I was initially told that the final meter reading had been physically provided by the tenant to then be told it had been automatically generated (read on). A hardline approach was adopted to try and get me to pay, which I resisted, and I was informed that, despite my complaint, I would continue to receive calls about the bill, and to ignore this contact.  It was stated, it would take 7-10 working days to hear from the compliant's team.
Since 6 March, I have received 31 calls and 11 text messages from Scottish Power.  Following their advice, I ignored most calls and messages, but when I did pick up (mainly out of frustration), without checking my account, the advisor went straight into their scripts requesting me to pay the bill.  Hugely intrusive and annoying.
The icing on the cake, was receiving an email last night threatening me with bailiff action.
Whilst typing this review, I have been on the phone to Scottish Power for 85 minutes (on hold for 40 minutes) in an effort to resolve the matter. I have been informed that my complaint that was lodged on 6 March actually hasn't been lodged, so I need to wait a further 7-10-working days to to hear back.  To stop and calls, texts, and threat of legal action, I have requested that my billing process is put of hold until I hear from the complaints team. I was initially informed that they do not have the ability to do this. Ridiculous right? You dispute an estimated bill, which is grossly incorrect, but you are still expected to pay it in the interim whilst the complaints team contact you. Bullyboy tactics in my opinion.
Having spoken to the Supervisor, Naveed, who has agreed to pause the billing process until my complaint is resolved, and cease all contact from the billing team, he was excellent, and his colleague Inga was lovely.  I wish I had spoken with both these individuals at the start of the process. During this call, it was confirmed that the final meter reading was physically provided by the tenant, contrary to one conversation on 6 March. I have been informed that my complaint has now been lodged and I will hear from them in 7-10 days (give or take).
I look forward to hearing from the complaints department in the hope that this horrendous situation can be resolved. 
For directors reading this review, please listen to the call recordings and you will understand my angst.  Happy to discuss your processes and customer service, which are hugely inefficient.

title: Scottish Power's Customer Service is Appalling!
content:
Communicating with Scottish power is HOPELESS! 
Their overseas? 'Help centre' is USELESS!

I tried for months to get any sense out of Scottish Power. 

In the end, I asked our local MP to intervene -- which he did and we finally gor a human to talk to!

The Problem: Our SMART meter didn't work. 

I raised a formal complaint. 

After weeks of waiting and missed appointments, they finally got a chap to come and add the 'Telemetry' unit to the so-called SMART meter -- on the second attempt as the first time, he didn't have the authority to add the unit, despite having them in his van! -- so had to go away and we had to wait another week for him to return to add the 4G telemetry device. 

After a further 2 weeks we all concluded it still didn't work!

Another Technician came and replaced everything -- SMART meter and Telemetry unit. This time it worked.

Lead time was around 2 months to get to this point.

We have a Heat Pump and wanted a reduced cost Heat pump tariff -- only to be told this week (24th March, now the SMART meter is working) that all heat pump tariffs have been withdrawn!
So the only option was to 'fix for 12 months.'
 
Goodness knows if and when they will introduce another Heat Pump Tariff.

The one glimmer of hope with Scottish Power is that once I raised a Formal Complaint and got a named complaint handler, communications improved and the problem of the SMART meter was resolved -- eventually!

Without the Customer going out of thier way to achive communication, Scottish Power's Customer Service is as close to 'Useless' as it is possible to get!

No one takes any Resposnsibility to solve the Customer's problem! You get passed from one unknowledgable person to the next -- until you put the phone down in frustration!

They don't have enough English speaking staff with sufficient understanding to be able to deal with a problem!

The on-line chat it worthless.

In truth, they don't appear to have enough staff and their 'on the road' contractor initally gave us a lead time of 6 months to change the SMART meter!
In reality the timescale was 2 months -- but only after I kept pushing them for a sensible date!

Until you raise a Formal Complaint, Scottish Power believe the problem is YOU!

We should not have to put up with such appalling service.

title: OMBUDSMAN complaint
content:
Title: Business account not set up – left with charges and no control

We are a business operating from 32 The Shambles, Worcester, and our experience with ScottishPower has been extremely poor.

From the start, we notified them that we had taken over the premises, provided full company details and meter readings, and requested an account to be set up correctly in our company name.

Despite this, ScottishPower failed to set up the account properly and refused to accept payments. This left us in a position where we were actively using electricity but had no account, no agreed tariff, and no way to manage or reduce any charges.

Over a period of approximately three months, charges were allowed to build up while we had no control over the situation. We were also unable to switch supplier during this time.

To make matters worse, debt collection correspondence has been sent relating to a previous occupier of the premises, which we have no connection to. We have had to formally dispute this.

We consider this completely unfair. If the account had been set up correctly from day one, we would have been able to manage the account, make payments, and choose an appropriate supplier.

This issue has now been formally raised as a complaint and escalated further.

Based on our experience, the handling of business accounts, particularly change of occupancy, needs significant improvement. Customers should not be left without control of their account while charges continue to accumulate.

title: I switched to ScottishPower from EDF…
content:
I switched to ScottishPower from EDF not long ago, and it has been the biggest mistake of my life. Since the switch, I have been harassed relentlessly—text messages, emails, phone calls—nonstop. If it’s not a text, it’s a call; if it’s not a call, it’s a letter. I have never encountered such aggressive payment chasing in my life. Constant calls, even from call centers abroad, all demanding payment for a trivial amount—around £100 for gas and electricity. I have mental health issues, and this constant bombardment has been absolutely unbearable.

In a bid to end this harassment, I spent over an hour on the phone to switch to prepayment—just to settle the small balance. But after all that effort, they admitted they couldn’t switch my gas supply, because their smart meter doesn’t detect it. Now, I’m left with a messed-up system—50% prepay, 50% direct debit—complete chaos. I asked for a technician to check the meter, but was told there are no appointments. They don’t care. They are in breach of regulations and they know it. This company has been an absolute nightmare. Do not—under any circumstances—switch to ScottishPower. You will regret it.

title: Absolute incompetence and a data protection nightmare! Avoid at all costs.
content:
I would give zero stars if I could. I moved into a newly converted studio flat on December 23rd, 2025, and my experience with Scottish Power has been nothing short of a documented nightmare.
 
When I called to register my account and provide my opening meter readings, I was horrified to find that they already had my personal details—but with a completely incorrect date of birth. Worse still, they had me registered as the responsible tenant for ALL 11 studio flats in the entire building!
 
They are trying to charge me for a massive bill dating back to August 2025. During that time, I wasn't even living in North London; I was living in Southwark. Despite me providing clear evidence, including my signed tenancy agreement proving my move-in date was 23rd December 2025, they have been utterly unhelpful.
 
Most concerning is that they refuse to explain how they obtained my personal information, or why they assigned an entire commercial-to-residential conversion to one single person. Their records are a mess, their data handling is suspicious, and their customer service is non-existent.
 
It’s no wonder this company has such a toxic reputation across social media. I am not the only resident in this building disgusted by their incompetence. I am switching suppliers immediately.

 Do yourself a favor and stay far away from Scottish Power. They are a disgrace.

title: Unnecessarily Inflated monthly payments
content: Let’s face it energy prices are not going to get cheaper soon and that’s not down to Scottish Power alone. However, they do like to rack up your monthly payments when you go a few pounds in debit over the winter but seem happy to see your credit increase to hundreds of pounds with no changes when your usage is lower in the Summar. It’s a simple calculation. How much have you spent on energy over the last the last 12 months? Divide that by 12, maybe add 5% for future increases, it’s not hard and their system actually does something similar.  But then they increase it automatically by much more, even though their system says you won’t spend that based on usage. Moral of the story? Don’t trust Scottish Power to get it right, check their calculations and adjust accordingly yourself. You should not have to do this, but Scottish Power obviously want more of your money earning interest for them.

title: Reimbursing wrongly applied debts.
content:
I have pre-payment smart meters that before this issue had never even gone into emergency credit, yet for the third year in a row some inexplicable debts have appeared. This happened in September of 2025 and I got in touch twice to ask for an explanation or rectify a mistake but nobody could provide an explanation or show me evidence that I actually owed the debt. Nobody ever got back in touch with me and it took until 3rd March 2026, when I called along with an advisor from CAB to get the issue sorted. As with the previous two years the money was refunded to my meters. It just took so much longer this time and I’m pretty sure that if I hadn’t enlisted the help of CAB I would have been ignored completely. 
So, I’m obviously happy with the outcome of 3rd March but I have to say that the performance of Scottish Power up until then falls far below the standard I would expect. This issue with the unexplained debts took over £60 from my meters between September and November last year, which coincided with another price increase and the onset of winter which has left me in fuel poverty, and the prices just keep increasing. Hence, despite the satisfactory outcome of the date mentioned above this can only ever be a mixed review. To put this in perspective; until the issue occurred I had not used my overdraft for years. Now I have two overdrafts and am pretty much living in them. This is what can happen when your energy supplier can take money from your meter that you don’t owe them and then hope that you don’t pursue the matter; it has a domino effect on the rest of your finances.

title: Unfortunately
content:
Unfortunately, communication within your company appears to be seriously lacking.
I have repeatedly informed your team that my property does not have a gas supply. This was first explained when I moved in back in October, and again in December when I received a gas bill on my account. I contacted you immediately, as this issue had already been clarified. Each time, I was met with confusion and asked, “What do you mean your house has oil and not gas?” requiring me to explain the situation again from the beginning.
In January, when my smart meter was due to be installed, the engineer arrived and the same discussion about a non-existent gas meter took place once more. After making several phone calls, he returned and informed me that he would proceed with installing the electricity smart meter but advised me to contact Scottish Power directly, as your records still incorrectly showed that I had a gas meter.
The level of customer service has been extremely disappointing, and the lack of communication between departments is particularly concerning.
Although the issue now appears to be resolved, this experience has significantly impacted my confidence in your service. Once my one-year contract comes to an end, I will be exploring alternative providers, not necessarily for a better price, but for a better standard of service.

Changed to Scottish power from octopus back in October 2024 when a really nice, polite young lad knocked on my door, I thought what the heck, I’ll give him a sale.
Fast forward 12 months I decided to switch back to Octopus, just because they provide more perks, somehow between Scottish power and octopus they only managed to switch my electric, I found this out when a representative from Scottish power rang me to say they would offer me £50 credit if I came back to Scottish power before my cooling off period with octopus ended, annoyed and confused with the situation I agreed.

Customer service agents I was told I just need to set the account up, oh and we have no knowledge of the £50 credit we offered you!

title: No gas for 3 days and no way to contact Scottish Power for the whole weekend
content:
I had no gas supply from early on Saturday morning, and tried contacting SP by phone. The offices were closed at the weekend and the message played advised me to use "chat" on the website. This I tried but to no avail. The answer to any questions I typed was "I have no answer to that question. When I wrote"I have no gas supply " I got the same response...."I have no answer to the question" I then noticed that everytime I typed another query, the panel showed "not sent" in red letters.
I eventually rang the National Gas Emergency Centre (who are available at the weekend) and explained that although it wasn't an emergency, I had no heating and no gas hob to use. The lady was extremely helpful and asked if I had checked my smart meter. This I went and did, only to find that it was dead, and therefore not allowing gas to flow. I would have to wait until 9am on Monday for the phone lines to open at SP. When I eventually got through to an agent, he was extremely polite and helpful. He arranged for an engineer to come out from a smart meter company within 3.5 hours. The engineer pointed out that my smart meters (gas and electric) were Stage 1 units and prone to problems. He was surprised however that the meter battery was completely empty and thus defunct, within a comparitively short period of time. Within 2 hours he had installed a new Stage 2 gas meter and connected a new communications module to the Stage 1 electric meter, so that the readings can be sent to SP. He did however advise me to contact SP agatand arrange for a Stage 2 electricity meter to be installed, where the new communications module can be used, thus allowing both gas and electricity to be sent simultaneously to SP. 
My complaint is the system that SP offer, to notify them of issues, is not fit for purpose, and the Stage 1 smart meters aren't fit for purpose either.
I have no complaint against the agent in Warrington who answered my call, not against the meter engineer who was very helpful and explained everything to me

title: Fed up with having to call to get Scottish Power to fix problems they themselves cause
content:
The lady I spoke to after receiving incorrect correspondence from Scottish Power (again) was very helpful.  As part of the bereavement team, that’s unsurprising, and I’ve found most people on that team to be very understanding on the phone. 
The automated systems, however, are in dire need of a serious overhaul. Failing to use information provided, continuing to chase outstanding bills despite telling us we don’t need to pay yet, sending “sorry you’re leaving us” letters (one for each of gas and electric in the same envelope) when some random (as far as you know) person asks to move the services, and after you yourself have cancelled it, are hopeless errors which are due to shoddy workmanship in the systems. It’s lazy, it’s unacceptable, and frankly most disappointing. Further… when dealing with a bereavement, it can be really quite upsetting. I don’t like being put in a position to have to make additional phone calls to get you to fix problems that should never have arisen if only your systems actually worked.

title: There is something badly wrong with the…
content: There is something badly wrong with the Scottish Power online account pages. Also, 'Password reset' simply does not work as one never receives the promised E-mail with the reset code.  Consequently, I've not been able to submit readings or review my direct debit payment until I serendipitously discovered that re-registering my  online account (despite already having an existing registratinon) was the key.  I'm resigned now to having to re-register every time I need to access my account.  Just to add insult, non of the fixed rate offers on the account page work and one is just taken in an endless circle of clicking an accept button that does not accept.  All in all, on-line account pages hedged with problems, albeit the lights still work so that, at least, service provision is excellent.
